### Post by az on 2005-05-13
How are you starting xfce?  Are you in X?

---

### Post by stevetone on 2005-05-13
[QUOTE=DaturaX]When i try to startxfce4, it gives me this error.

/usr/bin/startxfce4: Starting X server
/usr/bin/startxfce4: line 45: exec: xinit: not found[/QUOTE]

That error refers to a line in the script found at /usr/bin/startxfce4. On my system, that line is:

exec $prog /etc/xdg/xfce4/xinitrc $*

The variable $prog should be set to "xinit" at this point, thus the problem -- the program xinit is not found.

On my system, xinit is found in the /usr/bin/X11/ directory. Check to make sure it is there on your system. Also, check the value of $PATH -- the aforementioned directory that contains xinit should be in your path.

Although this is not a complete solution, I hope it starts pointing you in the right direction...

---

exec $prog /etc/xdg/xfce4/xinitrc $*

The variable $prog should be set to "xinit" at this point, thus the problem -- the program xinit is not found.

On my system, xinit is found in the /usr/bin/X11/ directory. Check to make sure it is there on your system. Also, check the value of $PATH -- the aforementioned directory that contains xinit should be in your path.

Although this is not a complete solution, I hope it starts pointing you in the right direction...[/QUOTE]

xinit is not found in my box /usr/bin/X11 directory. Now what should i do about this?
